toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet tests with blame-crash for dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet tests with blame-crash for dump collection

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. I need to:
1. Run the tests with `dotnet test`
2. Configure crash dump collection if the test host crashes

For .NET, crash dumps on host crash can be collected via:
- Environment variables: `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` and `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` to set the dump path
- The `--blame-crash` option in `dotnet test` which uses the blame data collector to capture crash dumps

The `--blame-crash` option is the standard way to collect crash dumps when a test host crashes. Let me use that.

arguments.description: Run dotnet tests with blame-crash for dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-869ec1cdc07347f0886763dcfaa8083a && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet tests with blame-crash for dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what I ran:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a crash dump (`.dmp` file) if the test host process crashes. Since no crash occurred, no dump was generated. If a crash does happen, the dump will be written to the `TestResults/` directory.
